characters.name: Auntie
characters.description: Auntieis aStoryperson. Auntie is a short-haired Japanese woman. Her apparel is a long-sleeved bright purple blouse, black trousers, purple socks and Mary Jane shoes.

characters.name: Auntie

characters.name: Boohbahs
characters.description: TheBoohbahsare furry atoms of energy, power, and light. Upon waking up in theirpodsin the magical, luminousBoohball, the colourful creatures known as the Boohbahs come to life, dancing and flying away in theBoohzone. They enjoy engaging in physical activity like dancing and exercising, and are powered into action by the laughter of the children playing with their Boohball. The most prominent part of a Boohbah's body is a large, round belly; a Boohbah is so fat that its flab oftentimes jiggles as it exercises or dances. Its body is very soft and fuzzy from neck to feet, giving it a "poofy" and cosy look, and sparkles with light. It has thick guts around its stomach area, cone-shaped arms and legs, feet which are similar in appearance to pointe shoes, three-fingered hands at the tips of its arms, and a neck similar in appearance to the collar of a roll-neck jumper (or turtleneck sweater). The Boohbahs are full of energy, both physically and mentally. They are always on the move - jumping, bumping, bouncing, spinning, shaking, falling (both down and up), etc. Like small children, they are also very hyperactive, often dissolving into a fast, free-moving frenzy in the middle of a dance or when exercising at full speed. One of the Boohbahs' most notable (and peculiar) traits is their ability to fly. Three times per episode, they soar up to the centre of the Boohzone and float either downward or upward, holding hands together in a circle like skydivers. While in this position, the Boohbahs do their power hum, with each one "singing" a musical note and sending a beam of light toward a central point (usually the Boohball). The first time they do the hum, they break up their circle and fly all over the place; the second time, they disappear into the Boohball before briefly reappearing to run around the Storypeople's present; the third time, they go back into the ball to nestle into their pods. To express themselves, the Boohbahs move their eyes to their left or right and hide their faces by retracting their heads into their hollow necks. They are mouthless and cannot speak (the only other onscreen characters, theStorypeople, don't speak either, even though theydohave mouths), but often while moving, they emit noises like squeals, flatulent air-bellowing poofs, and squeaks (their eyebrows make a whirring noise when they light up and their eyes click when they move).

characters.name: Brother and Sister
characters.description: Brother and SisterareStorypeople. They are a pair of teenagers. They are rarely, if ever, seen apart (for example, in "Windows" and "Drink of Milk", Brother is seen without Sister until Sister shows up). Brother and Sister are both very playful and energetic, as is evident while they come up with ideas to solve the situation they are faced with. If excited or pleased with the result of a solution, they do a "victory dance" to express their delight.

characters.name: Children
characters.description: In the beginning of every episode, aBoohballflew into two different countries (three in the US) around the world so it tickles the children as they play with it. They shush to theoffscreen childrennot to interrupt theBoohbahs. They took the present to theStorypeople, which is a surprise. When aBoohballapproaches the ground it left a rainbow swirl, so if some children can dance on it.

characters.name: Grandmamma
characters.description: Grandmammais aStoryperson. Grandmamma is an elderly-yet-spry woman. She has a greyish-white afro and wears a dark pink house dress, white sweater, a red skirt, white socks, and red (sometimes black) shoes.

characters.name: Grandmamma

characters.name: Grandpappa
characters.description: Grandpappais aStoryperson. Grandpappa is almost always trying to stop theStorypeople's pet,Little Dog Fido, from taking away things that he finds (like anarmchairor a pair ofslippers). He also has little luck getting inside certain objects, like ahammockor arocket.

characters.name: Little Dog Fido
characters.description: Little Dog Fidois theStorypeople's pet dog. He is also the onlyStoryworldcharacter known to make noise: he communicates by barking, like all dogs do. Fido is a Jack Russell Terrier; he has white fur with light brown spots and a bright red collar.

characters.name: Little Dog Fido

characters.name: Home
characters.description:
There are 314 articles on this wiki. Somebody's coming...
It's a Fandom user. Welcome to theBoohbahWiki, the unofficial guide to the one-of-a-kind interactive show by the same creator ofTeletubbies! We offer articles about your favourite episodes, as well as articles about characters, locations, and merchandise, so go do what you can do!

characters.name: Mr. Man
characters.description: Mr. Manis aStoryperson. Mr. Man typically wears a short-sleeved magenta shirt, white trousers, black socks and black sneakers. He is a husky black man.

characters.name: Mrs. Lady
characters.description: Mrs. Ladyis aStoryperson. Mrs. Lady is a heavyset Indian woman. She thinks she knows everything she does, but she is sometimes capable of going too far.

characters.name: Offscreen characters
characters.description: Since every onscreen character inBoohbahis nonverbal, a cast ofoffscreen characterscan be heard throughout the show directing them what to do or driving the narrative of a story along. There are two kinds of this sort of character: A group of childrenoften call theBoohbahsinto action numerous times during the first half of the show. They wake them up and call each one's name out to make them exercise, and when they lose control, try as hard as they can to get them in order (first by chanting "Boohbah!" and then by calling each Boohbah's name out again). After briefly stopping the Boohbahs, the children set them into motion again by shouting "Booh!" When a group of children appears onscreen todeliver a present to theStorypeople, the offscreen children are so excited about the present that they shout "Boohbah!" (the onscreen children often respond to this by shushing them so they don't disturb the Boohbahs). After the present is lifted into theBoohball, one of the offscreen children addresses it as being for the Storypeople and they all roll call the Storypeople's names. Later on, when the Storypeople run into a problem, the children send them another object to remedy it. After the story in Storyworld is over, the children can be heard giggling with glee, chanting "Booh! Bah! Booh! Bah! Booh!", and giggling once more as the scene fades back to theBoohzone.

characters.name: Storypeople
characters.description: TheStorypeopleare a diverse group of familial characters who live inStoryworld. The Storypeople are meant to be like pieces of a game that the children play and make stories with: one or more Storypeople may appear in an episode. The Storypeople are usually given an object and explore its use in a way that typically creates a problem, leaving it up to the children to send them another thing to resolve their issue. They don't live in any houses, but live outside in a giant isolated habitat surrounded by a few trees, beaches, and mountains. Most of the Storypeople are depicted as being happy-go-lucky and carefree (at least until a problem arises). Though they sometimes exhibit frustration or confusion, the Storypeople rarely, if ever, appear upset or sad. They do not speak - they interact and communicate by using pantomime. Most of the Storypeople are depicted as being happy-go-lucky and carefree (at least until a problem arises). Though they sometimes exhibit frustration or confusion, the Storypeople rarely, if ever, appear upset or sad. They do not speak - they interact and communicate by using pantomime.
